title: Missing access control on startAuction()
reason: Rejected as factually incorrect: `startAuction` is protected by `onlyOwner` at `Contract.sol:1232`.

title: Missing access control on endAuction()
reason: Rejected: permissionless settlement after expiry is an expected design pattern here and no realistic protocol harm was substantiated beyond the separate reentrancy issue already captured.

title: Missing access control on refundBids()
reason: Rejected: anyone triggering refunds after the auction ends is not inherently harmful; the actual reportable problems are the reentrancy and failed-transfer accounting bugs already captured.

title: buyNow() can mint NFT to zero address
reason: Rejected as unsupported: `buyNow` sets `highestBidder = _msgSender()` and mints to `_msgSender()`, never to `address(0)`.

title: Reentrancy vulnerability in ownerWithdraw()/ownerWithdrawTo()/ownerWithdrawAll()/ownerWithdrawAllTo()
reason: Rejected as an independent finding: these functions perform external calls, but the concrete exploitable issue is their missing access control, which is already captured in `F-001`.

title: editDuration() lacks zero value validation
reason: Rejected: this is an owner-controlled configuration choice, not a permissionless vulnerability causing realistic protocol-level harm.

title: Potential integer overflow in getBuyNowPrice()
reason: Rejected as incorrect under `pragma solidity ^0.8.15`, where arithmetic overflow reverts automatically.

title: editBaseUri() allows changing metadata at any time
reason: Rejected: owner-controlled metadata mutability is a trust/design concern, not a protocol exploit causing the required class of on-chain harm.

title: auctionStarted flag can be reset / auction cannot be restarted
reason: Rejected: inability to restart the one-off auction is a design choice, not a vulnerability.

title: Missing validation on bid function for zero-value bids
reason: Rejected as unsupported: `highestBid` starts at `0.9 ether` and `bid` requires `newBid >= highestBid + minBidStep`, so zero-value bids cannot satisfy the threshold.

title: Buy-now settlement can lock the NFT in contracts that cannot handle ERC721s
reason: Rejected: `buyNow` mints to `msg.sender`, so any lockup is self-inflicted by the buyer's chosen contract wallet rather than a protocol flaw creating realistic third-party harm.
